response:
to solve this problem we must first store the concept of prime factors involved in the factorial.
***
we need to store the count of prime factors of five within one hundred factorial to proceed.
***
the number of times five divides one hundred factorial is found by summing the quotients of division.
***
we calculate this sum to be twenty plus four which equals twenty four to store in our memory.
***
the denominator of the fraction involves powers of ten which are products of two and five.
***
we must store the fact that there are many more factors of two than factors of five present.
***
after dividing by ten to the power of fifty the remaining factors of five determine the denominator.
***
since we have twenty four fives and divide by fifty fives we are left with a deficit of twenty six.
***
we must store the result that the denominator is ten to the power of twenty six after simplification.
***
the final answer for the denominator of the irreducible fraction is ten raised to the twenty sixth power.
